evals.name: clipboard-snippets-extension
evals.prompt: I want to build a Raycast extension that lets me save text snippets and quickly paste them. I often copy code snippets, emails, or URLs throughout the day and want a way to store them with labels so I can find and paste them later. What should I build and how?
evals.expected_output: A Raycast extension with commands to list saved snippets (with search/filter), add new snippets via form, and paste/copy them. Should include a List view, Form view, use Cache for persistence, and proper ActionPanel with paste/copy actions.

assertions.name: produces_brainstorming
assertions.description: Output includes a brainstorming document with clarifying questions asked to the user before implementation

assertions.name: has_plan
assertions.description: Output includes a plan document outlining the extension structure before code

assertions.name: valid_manifest
assertions.description: package.json has valid Raycast manifest structure with commands array and proper mode values

assertions.name: uses_action_panel
assertions.description: Extension code uses ActionPanel/Action components for user interactions (paste, copy, delete)

assertions.name: uses_local_storage_or_cache
assertions.description: Extension uses LocalStorage or Cache API for persisting snippets

evals.name: api-status-menu-bar
evals.prompt: I need a Raycast menu bar extension that shows the status of my company's APIs. We have a health check endpoint at https://api.example.com/health that returns JSON like {"status":"ok","services":[{"name":"api","status":"up"},{"name":"db","status":"up"}]}. I want the menu bar icon to be green when all services are up, yellow if some are down, and red if the endpoint is unreachable. It should refresh every 5 minutes.
evals.expected_output: A menu-bar command extension with background refresh at 5-minute interval, useFetch for API calls, dynamic menu bar icon (Color/green/yellow/red), and a dropdown list showing service statuses.

assertions.name: produces_brainstorming
assertions.description: Output includes a brainstorming document with clarifying questions asked to the user before implementation

assertions.name: has_plan
assertions.description: Output includes a plan document outlining the extension structure before code

assertions.name: menu_bar_mode
assertions.description: Manifest specifies mode: 'menu-bar' for the command

assertions.name: has_interval
assertions.description: Manifest has interval: '5m' or equivalent 5-minute refresh setting

assertions.name: dynamic_icon
assertions.description: Code changes the menu bar icon color based on API status (green/yellow/red)

assertions.name: uses_fetch
assertions.description: Code uses useFetch or fetch to call the health check endpoint

evals.name: ai-commit-message-tool
evals.prompt: I want to create an AI extension tool for Raycast that generates git commit messages based on the git diff. The user should be able to @mention the extension in Quick AI and ask something like 'generate a commit message for my changes'. It should use the AI.ask API and read the git diff from the current directory.
evals.expected_output: An AI tool (not a regular command) that uses AI.ask to generate commit messages, reads git diff output, and is configured as a tool in the manifest with an ai.yaml for AI instructions.

assertions.name: produces_brainstorming
assertions.description: Output includes a brainstorming document with clarifying questions asked to the user before implementation

assertions.name: has_plan
assertions.description: Output includes a plan document outlining the extension structure before code

assertions.name: tool_definition
assertions.description: Manifest defines a tools array (not just commands) for AI tool usage

assertions.name: uses_ai_ask
assertions.description: Code uses AI.ask from @raycast/api to generate commit messages

assertions.name: has_ai_yaml
assertions.description: Includes an ai.yaml file with AI extension instructions for tool invocation context

assertions.name: reads_git_diff
assertions.description: Code reads git diff output (via child_process, exec, or similar) to get the changes
